title: React Compiler Analyzer — risk-detection config
description: Configuration for the `analyze --risk-config <file>` runtime-risk rules. See the package README ('Compiled but Risky') for rule semantics.

$schema.description: Optional reference to this JSON schema for editor IntelliSense.

storeAccessorPattern.description: Regex source matching store-accessor function names (e.g. `Store$` for `getAppStore`). When set, enables `getXStore().field` non-reactive snapshot detection (`nonreactive-store-read`, `medium`). Omit to disable.

detectGetStateReads.description: Enable detection of imperative `.getState()` snapshot reads (`nonreactive-store-read`, `high`). Default: false.

selectorHookProperties.description: Marker property names identifying a hidden selector hook accessed via property chain, e.g. ["use"] matches `store.use.field()` (`hidden-selector-hook`, `high`). Empty/omitted disables the rule.

resolveWrappers.description: Follow first-party wrapper calls and re-export barrels to flag risks reached indirectly (e.g. a component calling a plain helper that does `getStore().getState()`). Syntactic, first-party only — stops at node_modules, dynamic dispatch, and method calls on inferred receivers. Requires at least one leaf rule. Default: false.

pathAliases.description: tsconfig-style path aliases used by wrapper resolution to follow workspace imports (e.g. `@app/foo`).

baseUrl.description: Absolute directory the `paths` targets are resolved against.

paths.description: Map of alias pattern (e.g. `@app/*`) to target dirs (e.g. [`src/app/*`]).
